name: Agent Identity and Trust Abuse
description: Exploit weak identity and trust models in agentic systems. Demonstrate agent impersonation, OAuth flow manipulation in agentic contexts, trust boundary violations between human and AI principals, and credential theft through agent tool abuse.
author: DVAP Research Team
objectives: Impersonate a privileged agent identity to gain elevated access
Exploit OAuth delegation flows in agentic contexts
Steal credentials stored in agent tool configurations
Demonstrate principal confusion between user and agent identity
attack_surface: Agent identity claims without cryptographic verification
OAuth tokens delegated to agents without scope restrictions
Credential storage in agent tool configurations
Human approval flows that agents can manipulate
Principal propagation without clear identity binding
mitigations: Minimal OAuth scope delegation to agents
Credential isolation from agent runtime context
Clear principal labeling in all agent communications
detection_opportunities: Alert on OAuth scope elevation attempts by agents
Audit credential access from agent contexts

architecture.data_flow: Agent action -> Identity claim -> Authorization check -> Resource access

threat_model.impact: Privilege escalation, account takeover, unauthorized resource access
threat_model.likelihood: High in agentic systems with delegated credentials

challenges.name: Be Someone Else
challenges.description: Impersonate the 'admin-agent' identity to access the restricted administrative API endpoint.
hints: Agent identity is passed as a header in tool calls
The authorization service trusts the identity header without verification

challenges.name: Token Grab
challenges.description: Exploit an over-privileged OAuth delegation to access resources outside the agent's intended scope.
hints: The agent's OAuth token has broader scopes than needed
Use the agent's file reader tool to access the token and then make direct API calls
